question.text: An investor under the old income tax regime wants to save tax under Section 80C through mutual funds. Which scheme should the investor choose?

explanation.text:
**The Logic:** Among mutual funds, only ELSS qualifies for tax deduction under Section 80C of the Income Tax Act. The maximum deduction is Rs. 1.5 lakh per financial year. However, this benefit is available only under the old income tax regime. ELSS has a 3-year lock-in and minimum 80% investment in equity.
**The Trap:** Large cap funds, hybrid funds, and index funds do NOT qualify for Section 80C deduction regardless of how much you invest in them. Only ELSS has the specific tax-saving feature. Investors under the new tax regime cannot claim this deduction.
**Cross-Exam Context:** MF topics overlap with Series V-A, V-B. ELSS and its tax implications are heavily tested in all MF exams, especially with the new vs old tax regime distinction.
**Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Always check whether the client is on the old or new tax regime before recommending ELSS for tax saving. Under the new regime, there is no Section 80C benefit, so ELSS loses its tax advantage (though it remains a good equity fund with a 3-year lock-in).
